key_insight: Ultrasound- and microwave-assisted casein/gum arabic complexes, combined with niosome encapsulation, created effective layer-by-layer electrosprayed coatings that protected Bacillus coagulans and maximized viability.
engineering_relevance: The study shows a scalable ambient-temperature coating strategy for stabilizing sensitive bioactive particles, relevant to microencapsulation and advanced food/pharma processing workflows.
method: Ultrasonic pre-treatment followed by microwave-assisted glycation of casein/gum arabic at 150, 350, and 550 W
Preparation of Span 20- and Tween 80-based nanoniosomes to load Bacillus coagulans
Electrospraying of probiotic-loaded polymer solutions to form multilayer coated nanoparticles
evidence_signal: The 350 W complex gave the highest glycation and denaturation temperature, and 15% casein/gum arabic with Span 20 nanoniosomes achieved the highest post-electrospraying viability (97.85 ± 1.64%).
watch_item: Performance is formulation-sensitive, so the coating and viability gains may require careful control of microwave power, polymer concentration, and electrospraying conditions for scale-up.
paper_title: Layer-by-layer coating of electrosprayed Bacillus coagulans using niosomes and casein/gum arabic complexes induced by ultrasound and microwave-assisted interactions
paper_category: AI materials, welding, and machine design
